nodes.label: AI as debugging assistant
nodes.label_negative: AI as unreliable for complex debugging
nodes.grounding_span: cascading syntax errors, where a missing bracket earlier in the program can cause a syntax error way later in the program

nodes.label: AI as learning tool for terminology and understanding
nodes.label_negative: AI as hallucinator of non-existent terms
nodes.grounding_span: reverse dictionary search, where I can give a vague informal description of a topic, and ask what the proper name for it is

nodes.label: AI integration into workflow
nodes.grounding_span: I typically try to use it in such a way where I can return to using traditional workflows as soon as possible

nodes.label: AI reliability and factuality
nodes.grounding_span: hallucinated or extrapolated info, instead of just get being given a summary of the few fragments of info that are known

nodes.label: AI reliability for practical tasks
nodes.grounding_span: tools that focus less on presenting LLM generated text, and more on using LLMs to decide which human-authored text to present to you

nodes.label: Confidentiality in AI use
nodes.grounding_span: I avoid feeding code other people wrote into online LLMs because it feels quite awkward with the fact that these prompt may be used for training

nodes.label: Efficiency and time savings
nodes.grounding_span: One of the biggest timesaves I've gotten from LLMs

nodes.label: Deep understanding
nodes.grounding_span: fixing these errors myself usually gives me a deeper understanding of the code

nodes.label: Reliability and correctness
nodes.grounding_span: the error rate of current models is completely unacceptable for the vast majority of applications

nodes.label: Privacy and ethical data use
nodes.grounding_span: I can't consent to this on other people's behalf

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for time-saving
nodes.grounding_span: One of the biggest timesaves I've gotten from LLMs is, when working with an unfamiliar language, the ability to show it a snippet of syntax and have it give me keywords

nodes.label: Frustration with AI hallucination
nodes.grounding_span: One of the biggest frustrations and timewasters is when desperately looking for info that is obscure or possibly even non-existent, I will often get hallucinated or extrapolated info

nodes.label: Skepticism and caution toward AI
nodes.grounding_span: I don't think LLMs are actually very close to my skill level in the things that actually matter

nodes.label: Optimism about future AI integration
nodes.grounding_span: I hope we get more AI tools that integrate more tightly with traditional tools

nodes.label: Caution about privacy with sensitive information
nodes.grounding_span: The privacy concerns are fairly worrying

nodes.label: Systematic verification through source checking
nodes.grounding_span: I look for multiple independent uses of the term

nodes.label: Domain-specific tool reliance — prefers specialized software over general AI
nodes.grounding_span: I typically try to use it in such a way where I can return to using traditional workflows as soon as possible
